goal: Import a Franka Panda URDF into Isaac Sim 5.x and audit its collision meshes for oversized hulls, missing links, or wrong-axis joints.
thoughts: Maya is programmatic: prefer Python over GUI. import_robot wraps isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf (not the deprecated omni.isaac.urdf). Follow with verify_import because URDF imports routinely drop joint limits and produce single convex hulls on high-face meshes. A run_usd_script pass enumerates UsdPhysics.MeshCollisionAPI approximation per link so the 12,000-face gripper is visibly flagged.
code:
# 1. Import URDF via Isaac Sim 5.x importer
import_robot(
    urdf_path="<ABSOLUTE_URDF_PATH>",        # must be absolute, e.g. /home/maya/robots/franka_panda/panda.urdf
    dest_path="<STAGE_PRIM_PATH>",             # e.g. /World/Franka
    fix_base=True,
    merge_fixed_joints=False,
    convex_decomposition=False                 # keep default hulls, we audit next
)

# 2. Verify joints, mass, collision approximations
verify_import(prim_path="<STAGE_PRIM_PATH>")

# 3. Per-link collision audit
run_usd_script(script="""
from pxr import Usd, UsdGeom, UsdPhysics
stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
for prim in stage.Traverse():
    if prim.HasAPI(UsdPhysics.MeshCollisionAPI):
        api = UsdPhysics.MeshCollisionAPI(prim)
        approx = api.GetApproximationAttr().Get()
        mesh = UsdGeom.Mesh(prim)
        face_count = len(mesh.GetFaceVertexCountsAttr().Get() or [])
        bbox = UsdGeom.Imageable(prim).ComputeWorldBound(0, 'default').GetRange()
        print(prim.GetPath(), approx, 'faces=', face_count, 'size=', bbox.GetSize())
""")

# 4. If a link reports >255-face convex hull, flag it (PhysX limit) and consult docs
lookup_knowledge(query="convex decomposition vs convex hull PhysX Isaac Sim 5 URDF")
failure_modes: URDF path must be absolute, not ~-relative — home expansion fails inside kit
Deprecated 4.x omni.isaac.urdf calls break silently; always use isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf
Single convex hull on >255-face mesh compiles but crashes at PhysX simulate
Joint limits in URDF often omitted — verify_import must run before any training
